text.title: django-decision-tree Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for django-decision-tree as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Database query in utils/ (e.g., User.objects.filter() inside a utils function).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: utils/ functions have no DB dependency by convention. Adding ORM calls makes them non-pure, breaks unit testability (now you need a test DB), and blurs the service/util boundary. Any code needing a DB query belongs in services/, not utils/. Move the logic to services and have utils handle the pure transformation parts.

failure-mode.description: Business logic in views/ (conditional rules, calculations, multi-step DB operations directly in the view method).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Business logic in views is untestable without an HTTP context. The view becomes a 200-line method that is impossible to reuse or test in isolation. Agents writing new features will duplicate the logic in another view, creating inconsistency. Always delegate to services/.

failure-mode.description: Circular imports between apps (apps/orders/services.py importing from apps/users/services.py when users already imports orders).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Circular imports cause ImportError at startup, breaking the entire application. Root cause: tight coupling through direct imports. Solutions: break the import cycle by restructuring (extract shared code to core/), use Django signals for async notifications, or pass data by value instead of importing. Never accept circular imports; they're always a design smell.

failure-mode.description: Service importing a view (to call view methods or access view constants).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: A service should not know about HTTP. If you need to import a view in a service, that's a sign the code should be in utils or the view should delegate to the service instead.

failure-mode.description: Util importing a service (for convenience, to avoid passing parameters).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Utils are pure helpers. If a util imports a service, it's no longer pure and loses testability. Break the util into smaller pure functions and have the service call them.
